text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY -- FOLLOW-UP VISIT
Completion of Neoadjuvant Therapy / Pre-Surgical Assessment

ATTENDING: [Attending Oncologist]
REFERRING PROVIDER: [Surgical Oncology]

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Ms. Okonkwo is a 38-year-old woman with stage IIB (T2N1M0) left breast triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma, grade 3, germline BRCA1 p.E1250* mutation carrier, presenting today for end-of-neoadjuvant-therapy assessment following completion of the full KEYNOTE-522 inspired regimen. She is here for physical exam, toxicity review, pre-surgical planning, and discussion of next steps.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Okonkwo originally presented approximately 5 months ago with a self-detected left breast mass. Workup confirmed a 3.1 cm left upper outer quadrant mass (10 o'clock position, 3 cm from nipple) with a single enlarged left axillary lymph node (2.0 cm). Core biopsy confirmed grade 3 invasive ductal carcinoma: ER 0%, PR 0%, HER2 1+ by IHC (triple-negative). Ki-67 was strikingly high at 85%. PD-L1 CPS 15 (22C3). TILs 40%. Germline testing reflexed from tumor NGS confirmed a pathogenic BRCA1 variant (c.3750G>A, p.E1250*). Staging CT and bone scan showed no distant metastatic disease. She was staged cT2N1M0, Stage IIB.

She was enrolled on a neoadjuvant regimen consisting of pembrolizumab 200 mg IV q3weeks plus dose-dense doxorubicin/cyclophosphamide (ddAC) q2 weeks x 4 cycles, followed by weekly paclitaxel 80 mg/m2 x 12 weeks with concurrent pembro q3 weeks. Pegfilgrastim was administered on day 2 of each ddAC cycle for primary febrile neutropenia prophylaxis.

Her course was notable for the following:

1. Grade 2 immune-mediated hepatitis, occurring during ddAC cycle 3 (approximately 5 weeks into therapy). ALT peaked at 186 U/L (~4x ULN), AST 152 U/L. She was asymptomatic. Pembrolizumab was held; prednisone 1 mg/kg/day was started. LFTs normalized within 3 weeks. Pembro was resumed after confirmed normalization. Infectious and autoimmune hepatitis workup was negative (HBV/HCV negative, ASMA negative, ANA 1:80 speckled -- likely low-titer nonspecific). ddAC cycles were administered on schedule throughout.

2. Grade 2 CINV during ddAC cycle 2 despite standard triple-antiemetic prophylaxis (ondansetron, dexamethasone, aprepitant); required IV hydration in the infusion clinic once. Anti-emetics were augmented with olanzapine 10 mg on days 1-4 for subsequent cycles with good effect.

3. Grade 2 complete alopecia, as anticipated with doxorubicin-based therapy, beginning after cycle 1.

4. Mid-treatment breast MRI after ddAC x 4 cycles (prior to initiating taxane): primary mass decreased from 3.1 cm to 1.8 cm; involved axillary node down from 2.0 cm to 1.0 cm with partial return of fatty hilum -- partial response documented.

5. Grade 2 peripheral sensory neuropathy developing during week 8 of 12 of weekly paclitaxel, manifesting as bilateral hand and foot numbness and tingling with impaired fine motor function (difficulty buttoning clothes, difficulty using a keyboard). Paclitaxel was dose-reduced by 20% to 64 mg/m2 for the remaining 4 doses. Duloxetine was started at 30 mg/day, titrated to 60 mg/day. Neuropathy has improved to grade 1 by today's visit.

She has now completed: ddAC x 4 cycles + pembrolizumab x 4 doses, followed by weekly paclitaxel x 12 (12 total infusions, last 4 at 64 mg/m2) + pembrolizumab x 4 additional doses (8 pembrolizumab cycles total in the neoadjuvant phase). She received her last paclitaxel infusion approximately 2.5 weeks ago and her last pembro approximately 2 weeks ago.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Fatigue: mild, grade 1, improved significantly over the past 2 weeks since completing taxane. She describes it as manageable.
Nausea: resolved.
Peripheral neuropathy: bilateral feet, mild tingling and numbness, grade 1. Hands are substantially better -- she is now able to use a keyboard and button clothes again. No burning pain.
Alopecia: complete, grade 2. She is using a wig and coping well.
Musculoskeletal: mild arthralgias, consistent with post-paclitaxel myalgias, improving.
Left breast: she notices the mass feels smaller than at the start of treatment. Mild residual firmness.
Mood: anxious about surgery and surgical outcome. She has been reading about pCR rates and is asking about what residual disease means for prognosis.
Sleep: mildly disrupted.
No dyspnea, chest pain, fever, rash, jaundice, bowel changes, or new bone pain.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
General: Well-appearing, alert, and oriented woman in no acute distress. Wearing a wig. Mild pallor.
Vitals: Blood pressure 118/74 mmHg, heart rate 78 bpm, temperature 98.2°F, weight 63.2 kg (baseline 65.1 kg, 3% weight loss since treatment start -- clinically insignificant).
Performance status: ECOG 0-1 (some residual fatigue but fully ambulatory and functional).

Head/Neck: No scalp or facial lesions. No palpable cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.

Breast: Left breast -- lumpectomy has not yet been performed; this is pre-surgical examination. Palpable residual mass in the left upper outer quadrant, approximately 1.5 cm by clinical exam, slightly firm with poorly defined margins. The mass is less defined than at initial presentation, likely representing treatment effect. No skin changes (no erythema, no peau d'orange, no satellite nodules). No nipple retraction or discharge. Right breast: no palpable mass or dominant nodule.

Axilla: Left axillary lymph nodes not palpable (previously palpable 2 cm node at presentation). Right axilla: no adenopathy.

Heart: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs. Given prior doxorubicin exposure (total 4 cycles), cardiac function was assessed; MUGA performed 4 weeks ago showed LVEF 58% (baseline 62%), clinically acceptable.

Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezing (patient has a history of mild intermittent asthma; albuterol PRN; no exacerbations during treatment).

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly. No masses.

Extremities: No edema. No lymphedema of the left upper extremity.

Neurological: Decreased light touch sensation bilateral feet (toes and soles), symmetric. Proprioception intact. Grip strength full bilaterally. DTRs intact. No gait abnormality. Consistent with grade 1 peripheral sensory neuropathy.

Skin: No new rashes, no palmar-plantar changes. Scalp without lesions.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (TODAY)
CBC:
  WBC 5.8 x10^3/uL (within normal limits)
  Hemoglobin 11.4 g/dL (mildly low; baseline 13.2, likely chemotherapy-related anemia -- grade 1)
  Platelets 242 x10^3/uL (normal)
  ANC 3.2 x10^3/uL (adequate)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
  ALT 22 U/L (normal; fully normalized after prior irAE hepatitis)
  AST 19 U/L (normal)
  Total bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL (normal)
  Alkaline phosphatase 72 U/L (normal)
  Creatinine 0.78 mg/dL (normal)
  Albumin 3.9 g/dL

TSH: 3.1 mIU/L (normal -- baseline, not yet on levothyroxine; thyroid function currently intact)
Free T4: 1.1 ng/dL (normal)

---

IMAGING
Pre-surgical breast MRI reviewed (obtained approximately 3-4 weeks ago after completion of the taxane/pembro phase, per standard institutional protocol for pre-surgical planning): The primary left breast mass measures approximately 1.4-1.6 cm in longest dimension, with heterogeneous enhancement -- representing substantial reduction from the initial 3.1 cm mass at diagnosis. The left axillary lymph node is no longer pathologically enlarged; no discrete cortical thickening or loss of fatty hilum. Post-treatment changes noted. Overall impression: continued partial response to neoadjuvant therapy. No new lesions.

Note: Formal imaging report was reviewed separately. No evidence of distant disease on prior staging workup.

---

ASSESSMENT

1. Left breast invasive ductal carcinoma, triple-negative, grade 3, cT2N1M0 -- Status post completion of full neoadjuvant regimen (ddAC x 4 cycles + pembro x 8 cycles + weekly paclitaxel x 12 doses). Clinical and radiographic partial response. Residual palpable left breast mass ~1.5 cm; left axillary adenopathy clinically resolved. Excellent tolerance of the full neoadjuvant course overall.

   Despite the encouraging axillary response, residual breast tumor is palpable, indicating this is not a clinical complete response. Path CR rates with KEYNOTE-522 in TNBC are in the 60-65% range; she has responded but the picture is consistent with residual disease. This has implications for adjuvant planning (CREATE-X data, potential olaparib consideration given BRCA1 mutation status).

2. Grade 1 peripheral sensory neuropathy (bilateral feet), improving. Duloxetine 60 mg/day continued.

3. Germline BRCA1 p.E1250* -- Genetic counseling referral placed at initial consultation; patient has been seen. Fertility preservation was discussed at the outset; patient deferred cryopreservation. Risk-reducing BSO deferred for later consideration.

4. Mild chemotherapy-related anemia, grade 1 -- Hemoglobin 11.4 g/dL, asymptomatic. No transfusion or erythropoiesis-stimulating agent indicated at this level.

5. Prior grade 2 immune-mediated hepatitis (pembrolizumab-related), resolved -- LFTs normal today. Prednisone taper completed.

---

PLAN

1. Definitive surgery: Referral to surgical oncology is active. Plan is for left lumpectomy with wire localization of the residual tumor bed (clip placed at time of biopsy) and sentinel lymph node biopsy. If intraoperative frozen section confirms sentinel node involvement, proceed to axillary lymph node dissection (levels I/II) per institutional protocol. Surgical margins and full specimen to be sent for complete pathologic evaluation including assessment of residual disease and RCB calculation. Pembrolizumab will be held in the perioperative period per institutional protocol (last dose was approximately 2 weeks ago; surgery to be scheduled within the next 2-3 weeks -- timing acceptable).

2. Post-surgical planning discussion (pending path results):
   - If pCR (ypT0/is ypN0): will resume pembrolizumab for completion of planned 1-year total course (approximately 9 additional adjuvant cycles), in line with KEYNOTE-522 adjuvant data.
   - If residual invasive disease (non-pCR): adjuvant capecitabine per CREATE-X will be strongly considered in addition to completing pembrolizumab; additionally, given germline BRCA1 mutation, olaparib may be considered as an alternative or sequential strategy -- will discuss at tumor board and with patient following final pathologic assessment. The OlympiA trial data is relevant here (adjuvant olaparib in germline BRCA1/2, high-risk HER2-negative with residual disease or high risk at presentation).
   - Adjuvant whole breast radiation will be planned regardless (given lumpectomy), with regional nodal irradiation per standard indications based on final pathologic axillary assessment.

3. Neuropathy: Continue duloxetine 60 mg/day. Re-evaluate at post-surgical visit. Functional improvement noted; do not expect significant worsening off paclitaxel.

4. BRCA1 implications: Patient counseled today again regarding elevated lifetime risk of ovarian cancer. RRSO is recommended between ages 35-40 per NCCN guidelines for BRCA1 carriers; will revisit this discussion once she is through surgery and recovery. Gynecology/gynecologic oncology referral offered.

5. Supportive care: Albuterol inhaler PRN for asthma (no changes needed). Multivitamin continued. Nausea largely resolved off active chemotherapy; ondansetron PRN as needed.

6. Return to clinic: Post-operatively in 3-4 weeks for wound check, pathology review, and adjuvant treatment planning. Patient given instructions to call with fever, worsening pain, neurologic changes, or concerns.

Case will be re-presented at multidisciplinary breast tumor board following surgical pathology results.

Time spent with patient: approximately 45 minutes, including counseling and coordination of care.

[Electronically signed by Attending Oncologist]
